data.title: Non-text Content
data.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.

data.title: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
data.description: For prerecorded audio-only and prerecorded video-only media, an alternative is provided that presents equivalent information.

data.title: Captions (Prerecorded)
data.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

data.title: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
data.description: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media.

data.title: Captions (Live)
data.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

data.title: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
data.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

data.title: Sign Language (Prerecorded)
data.description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

data.title: Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
data.description: Where pauses in foreground audio are insufficient to allow audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video, extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

data.title: Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
data.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media and for all prerecorded video-only media.

data.title: Audio-only (Live)
data.description: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.

data.title: Info and Relationships
data.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

data.title: Meaningful Sequence
data.description: When the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

data.title: Sensory Characteristics
data.description: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

data.title: Orientation
data.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

data.title: Identify Input Purpose
data.description: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined when the input field serves a purpose identified in the Input Purposes for User Interface Components section.

data.title: Identify Purpose
data.description: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.

data.title: Use of Color
data.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.

data.title: Audio Control
data.description: If any audio on a Web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio, or a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume level.

data.title: Contrast (Minimum)
data.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, except for large text, incidental text, and logotypes.

data.title: Resize Text
data.description: Except for captions and images of text, text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality.

data.title: Images of Text
data.description: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation, text is used to convey information rather than images of text.

data.title: Contrast (Enhanced)
data.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1, except for large text, incidental text, and logotypes.

data.title: Low or No Background Audio
data.description: For prerecorded audio-only content that contains primarily speech in the foreground, background sounds are at least 20 dB lower than the foreground speech content, or can be turned off.

data.title: Visual Presentation
data.description: For the visual presentation of blocks of text, a mechanism is available to select foreground and background colors, width is no more than 80 characters, text is not justified, line spacing is at least space-and-a-half, and text can be resized up to 200 percent.

data.title: Images of Text (No Exception)
data.description: Images of text are only used for pure decoration or where a particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed.

data.title: Reflow
data.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for vertical scrolling content at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels, and for horizontal scrolling content at a height equivalent to 256 CSS pixels.

data.title: Non-text Contrast
data.description: The visual presentation of user interface components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent colors.

data.title: Text Spacing
data.description: No loss of content or functionality occurs when users override text spacing properties: line height to at least 1.5 times the font size, spacing following paragraphs to at least 2 times the font size, letter spacing to at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing to at least 0.16 times the font size.

data.title: Content on Hover or Focus
data.description: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden, the additional content is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent.

data.title: Keyboard
data.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes.

data.title: No Keyboard Trap
data.description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component of the page using a keyboard interface, then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface.

data.title: Keyboard (No Exception)
data.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes.

data.title: Character Key Shortcuts
data.description: If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter, punctuation, number, or symbol characters, then a mechanism is available to turn off the shortcut, remap it, or make it active only on focus.

data.title: Timing Adjustable
data.description: For each time limit that is set by the content, the user can turn off, adjust, or extend the time limit.

data.title: Pause, Stop, Hide
data.description: For moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating information, a mechanism is available for the user to pause, stop, or hide it.

data.title: No Timing
data.description: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content, except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events.

data.title: Interruptions
data.description: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency.

data.title: Re-authenticating
data.description: When an authenticated session expires, the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating.

data.title: Timeouts
data.description: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss, unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions.

data.title: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
data.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period, or the flash is below the general flash and red flash thresholds.

data.title: Three Flashes
data.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.

data.title: Bypass Blocks
data.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple Web pages.

data.title: Page Titled
data.description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose.

data.title: Focus Order
data.description: If a Web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

data.title: Link Purpose (In Context)
data.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context.

data.title: Multiple Ways
data.description: More than one way is available to locate a Web page within a set of Web pages except where the Web Page is the result of, or a step in, a process.

data.title: Headings and Labels
data.description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

data.title: Focus Visible
data.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

data.title: Location
data.description: Information about the user's location within a set of Web pages is available.

data.title: Link Purpose (Link Only)
data.description: A mechanism is available to allow the purpose of each link to be identified from link text alone.

data.title: Section Headings
data.description: Section headings are used to organize the content.

data.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
data.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content.

data.title: Focus Not Obscured (Enhanced)
data.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden by author-created content.

data.title: Focus Appearance
data.description: When the keyboard focus indicator is visible, an area of the focus indicator meets a minimum size and has a sufficient contrast ratio.

data.title: Pointer Gestures
data.description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture.

data.title: Pointer Cancellation
data.description: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer, at least one of the following is true: the down-event is not used, completion can be aborted or undone, the up-event reverses the down-event, or completing the function on the down-event is essential.

data.title: Label in Name
data.description: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually.

data.title: Motion Actuation
data.description: Functionality that can be operated by device motion or user motion can also be operated by user interface components and responding to the motion can be disabled.

data.title: Target Size (Enhanced)
data.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels except when the target is available through an equivalent link or control, the target is in a sentence or block of text, the size is determined by the user agent, or a particular presentation is essential.

data.title: Concurrent Input Mechanisms
data.description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform except where the restriction is essential, required to ensure the security of the content, or required to respect user settings.

data.title: Dragging Movements
data.description: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation can be achieved by a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential or the functionality is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author.

data.title: Target Size (Minimum)
data.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, except where an equivalent target is provided, the target is in a sentence or block of text, the size is determined by the user agent, or a particular presentation is essential.

data.title: Language of Page
data.description: The default human language of each Web page can be programmatically determined.

data.title: Language of Parts
data.description: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined.

data.title: Unusual Words
data.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon.

data.title: Abbreviations
data.description: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available.

data.title: Reading Level
data.description: When text requires reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level after removal of proper names and titles, supplemental content, or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available.

data.title: Pronunciation
data.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation.

data.title: On Focus
data.description: When any user interface component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context.

data.title: On Input
data.description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component.

data.title: Consistent Navigation
data.description: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple Web pages within a set of Web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated.

data.title: Consistent Identification
data.description: Components that have the same functionality within a set of Web pages are identified consistently.

data.title: Change on Request
data.description: Changes of context are initiated only by user request or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes.

data.title: Consistent Help
data.description: If a Web page contains help mechanisms that are repeated on multiple Web pages within a set of Web pages, they occur in the same relative order to other page content.

data.title: Error Identification
data.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item that is in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text.

data.title: Labels or Instructions
data.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

data.title: Error Suggestion
data.description: If an input error is automatically detected and suggestions for correction are known, then the suggestions are provided to the user.

data.title: Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
data.description: For Web pages that cause legal commitments or financial transactions for the user to occur, that modify or delete user-controllable data in data storage systems, or that submit user test responses, submissions are reversible, checked, or confirmed.

data.title: Help
data.description: Context-sensitive help is available.

data.title: Error Prevention (All)
data.description: For Web pages that require the user to submit information, submissions are reversible, checked, or confirmed.

data.title: Redundant Entry
data.description: Information previously entered by or provided to the user that is required to be entered again in the same process is either auto-populated or available for the user to select.

data.title: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
data.description: A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless that step provides at least one alternative or assistance mechanism.

data.title: Accessible Authentication (Enhanced)
data.description: A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless that step provides at least one alternative that does not rely on a cognitive function test.

data.title: Parsing (Obsolete and removed)
data.description: This criterion was originally adopted to address problems that assistive technology had directly parsing HTML. Assistive technology no longer has any need to directly parse HTML. Consequently, these problems either no longer exist or are addressed by other criteria. This criterion no longer has utility and is removed.

data.title: Name, Role, Value
data.description: For all user interface components, the name and role can be programmatically determined; states, properties, and values that can be set by the user can be programmatically set; and notification of changes to these items is available to user agents, including assistive technologies.

data.title: Status Messages
data.description: In content implemented using markup languages, status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties such that they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus.
